sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
LEVEL 1 COMPLETED! The hypothesis was correct: the goal is to align the YELLOW TAIL with the YELLOW GAP by moving the agent left/right using the blue blocks.
Now I'm on Level 2. Let me inspect the new board to understand the layout and apply the same strategy.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# LEVEL 1 COMPLETED! Goal: align yellow tail with yellow gap by moving agent left/right.
# Now on Level 2. Let me inspect the new board.

seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Level 2 objects:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    print(f"  color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, bbox=[{min(rows)},{min(cols)}]-[{max(rows)},{max(cols)}]")

# Find agent, yellow tail, yellow gap, and blue blocks
agent = None
yellow_tail = None
yellow_gap = None
blue_blocks = []

for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] == 16:
        agent = n
    elif n['color'] == 'Y':
        b = n['boundary']
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        if min(cols) >= 40:  # tail is on the right side
            yellow_tail = n
        else:
            yellow_gap = n
    elif n['color'] == 'b':
        blue_blocks.append(n)

print(f"\nAgent: {agent['boundary'] if agent else 'None'}")
print(f"Yellow tail: {yellow_tail['boundary'] if yellow_tail else 'None'}")
print(f"Yellow gap: {yellow_gap['boundary'] if yellow_gap else 'None'}")
print(f"Blue blocks: {[b['boundary'] for b in blue_blocks]}")

# Calculate how many LEFT moves needed to align yellow tail with yellow gap
if agent and yellow_tail and yellow_gap:
    tail_cols = [p[1] for p in yellow_tail['boundary']]
    gap_cols = [p[1] for p in yellow_gap['boundary']]
    tail_left = min(tail_cols)
    gap_left = min(gap_cols)
    diff = tail_left - gap_left
    moves = diff // 4  # Each move shifts agent by 4 columns
    print(f"\nTail left col: {tail_left}, Gap left col: {gap_left}")
    print(f"Difference: {diff} columns")
    print(f"Moves needed: {moves} (LEFT if positive, RIGHT if negative)")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! Level 2 has a different layout:
- The board is mostly gray (G) with white (W) regions
- There are 4 blue blocks on the LEFT edge (cols 0-3)
- The agent is at [52,10]-[57,13] (charcoal, 16 pixels)
- There's a light green (N) object at [40,28]-[43,29] (8 pixels) - this might be the "gap"
- There's another light green (N) object at [52,8]-[57,9] (12 pixels) - this might be the "tail"
- No yellow objects this time!
So the colors have changed:
- Level 1: yellow gap (Y, 8 pixels), yellow tail (Y, 12 pixels)
- Level 2: light green gap (N, 8 pixels), light green tail (N, 12 pixels)
The pattern is the same: align the tail with the gap by moving the agent left/right.
- Tail: [52,8]-[57,9], cols 8-9
- Gap: [40,28]-[43,29], cols 28-29
- Tail needs to move RIGHT by (28-8) = 20 columns
- 20 / 4 = 5 moves RIGHT
But wait, the blue blocks are on the LEFT edge (cols 0-3), not the right edge. There are 4 blue blocks at rows 16-19, 24-27, 36-39, and 44-47. In Level 1, there were 2 blue blocks: upper and lower. Clicking the upper blue block moved the agent RIGHT, and clicking the lower blue block moved the agent LEFT.
In Level 2, there are 4 blue blocks. I need to figure out which one moves the agent RIGHT and which one moves the agent LEFT. Let me think about this...
In Level 1:
- Upper blue block (rows 24-27) = RIGHT
- Lower blue block (rows 32-35) = LEFT
In Level 2, there are 4 blue blocks at rows 16-19, 24-27, 36-39, and 44-47. Maybe:
- Top 2 blue blocks = RIGHT
- Bottom 2 blue blocks = LEFT
Or maybe:
- Odd-indexed blue blocks = RIGHT
- Even-indexed blue blocks = LEFT
Actually, let me think about this differently. In Level 1, the blue blocks were on the RIGHT edge. In Level 2, they're on the LEFT edge. Maybe the behavior is reversed:
- Upper blue blocks = LEFT
- Lower blue blocks = RIGHT
Or maybe the behavior is the same:
- Upper blue blocks = RIGHT
- Lower blue blocks = LEFT
Let me test by clicking on the topmost blue block (rows 16-19) and see which direction the agent moves.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Level 2 analysis:
# - Agent at [52,10]-[57,13] (cols 10-13)
# - Green tail at [52,8]-[57,9] (cols 8-9)
# - Green gap at [40,28]-[43,29] (cols 28-29)
# - 4 blue blocks on LEFT edge at rows 16-19, 24-27, 36-39, 44-47
#
# Tail needs to move RIGHT by 20 columns (from col 8 to col 28)
# 20 / 4 = 5 moves RIGHT
#
# Need to find which blue block moves agent RIGHT
# In Level 1: upper blue = RIGHT, lower blue = LEFT
# In Level 2: blue blocks are on LEFT edge, not right edge
# Let me test the topmost blue block

print("Testing topmost blue block (row 17, col 1) to see direction...")
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 17, 'col': 1}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
